article_title: OpenAI Pushes Codex Beyond Code Into Finance and Legal, Squaring Off Against Anthropic's Claude for Legal

content_preview.title: OpenAI Pushes Codex Beyond Code Into Finance and Legal, Squaring Off Against Anthropic's Claude for Legal
content_preview.summary: OpenAI is extending Codex into finance and legal work, weeks after Anthropic expanded Claude for Legal with 12 plugins and 20-plus integrations.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The race to embed generative AI inside specific professional industries has opened a new front. OpenAI is expanding Codex, originally a software-coding tool, into business verticals such as finance and law, according to [The Tech Portal](https://thetechportal.com/2026/06/02/openai-launches-new-codex-plugins-plans-expansion-into-finance-and-legal-services/). The move follows Anthropic, which weeks earlier mounted what it called its most expansive push yet into legal workflows, as rep...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, well-structured Analysis (847 words) on the OpenAI-vs-Anthropic competition in professional verticals, with a clear-eyed reliability section and disciplined single-outlet attribution throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (The Tech Portal) confirms the five Codex plugin names (Corporate Finance, Private Equity Investing, Marketing Strategy, Strategy Consulting, Legal), all seven data providers (Moody's, FactSet, LSEG, PitchBook, Daloopa, Datasite, S&P), and the 'under development' status. source-2 (Fortune) confirms the May 12 release, '20 new integrations', '12 role-specific' plugins, the Microsoft 365 tie-in, the four named firms (Freshfields, Quinn Emanuel, Holland & Knight, Crosby Legal), the Sullivan & Cromwell hallucination, the Claude Opus 4.7 90.9% BigLaw Bench score, and the Jay Madheswaran (Eve) 'an authoritative-sounding hallucination is worse than no answer' quote. source-1 (TechCrunch) confirms Claude for Legal, the clerical-function list, the Harvey $200M / $11B March raise, the Legora $600M Series D, and the Anthropic spokesperson 'mounting pressure to adopt AI' quote.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every plugin name, data provider, law firm, dollar figure, benchmark, and both quotes are verbatim in the cited outlet. The bot made a sound editorial call to reframe the piece around the allowlisted, fully-fetched facts and to exclude the OpenAI/Boehmig 'Codex for Legal' hire, which is carried only by bot-blocked / non-allowlisted outlets (Bloomberg 403). No fabricated specifics.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical; the 'Reliability Problem' section and the Analysis are clearly labeled and grounded in cited reporting rather than speculation.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — distinct AI-vertical-competition Analysis with no prior coverage of this OpenAI Codex finance/legal expansion.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified Analysis with model attribution discipline and prudent exclusion of unverifiable claims. APPROVE.
